# TATA  KFS and Agreement Phase 1

: Ayush Kumar
Created time: September 19, 2024 4:06 PM
Status: In progress
Last edited: November 18, 2024 1:08 PM
Owner: Ayush Kumar

# **What problem are we solving?**

RBI guidelines requires that lenders and LSP showcase the KFS format as specified. While the KFS is designed keeping borrower protection in mind, handling it in a elegant way without compromising on the experience is a challenge. 

---

# **How do we measure success?**

We intend to measure the success rate using the below metrics.

- Acceptance rate of atleast 95%
- Acceptance TAT from rendering of <30s
- Number of tickets related to KFS to be <5% of the KFS step applications

---

# **How are others solving this problem?**

Most lenders are solving this problem in 1 of the ways.

- Render as a full PDF or document object
- Render as a elegant UI like we have done for DSP
- Render the key items on UI elegantly with a detailed document

---

# **What is the solution?**

## Requirements overview (optional)

We are breaking down the requirement from TATA into 2 phase.

- Phase 1: in this phase, we will only modify the agreement since Tata is good with this
- Phase 2: As soon as Phase 1 is deployed to UAT, we will modify the KFS in one of the ways depending on the effort and faster go-live.
    - Replicate the DSP UI OR
    - Pass the HTML/PDF from BE

<aside>
💡 Considering the impact on business, we intend to focus on the agreement rendering than KFS changes in phase 1

</aside>

## User stories / User flow

Below is the customer journey.

Step 1: customer lands on the current page after completing the pledge step

![Screenshot 2024-09-27 at 12.30.23 AM.png](TATA%20KFS%20and%20Agreement%20Phase%202/Screenshot_2024-09-27_at_12.30.23_AM.png)

Step 2: customer views the KFS as it is currently and consents. This will be changed in phase 2. In parallel, we hit TATA’s APIs and the customer is redirected to the next page

![Screenshot 2024-09-27 at 12.32.04 AM.png](TATA%20KFS%20and%20Agreement%20Phase%202/Screenshot_2024-09-27_at_12.32.04_AM.png)

Step 3: customer can see the agreement landing page where they click on the button. At this stage, we hit the Signdesk API with the updated agreement template

![Screenshot 2024-09-27 at 12.32.17 AM.png](TATA%20KFS%20and%20Agreement%20Phase%202/Screenshot_2024-09-27_at_12.32.17_AM.png)

Step 4: customer sees the loader as mentioned below

![Screenshot 2024-09-27 at 12.34.55 AM.png](TATA%20KFS%20and%20Agreement%20Phase%201/Screenshot_2024-09-27_at_12.34.55_AM.png)

Step 5: customer is redirected to the agreement page generated by TATA through Signdesk as below.

Step 6: customer is redirected back to the page in case of app or closes the window and sees the page below after we receive a callback from TATA.

 

## Requirements

Changes in agreement to be documented : 

[Agreement_changes_comparision.pdf](TATA%20KFS%20and%20Agreement%20Phase%201/Agreement_changes_comparision.pdf)

### **Fresh Agreement Changes**

KFS Section: this section is a completely new section that will be added to the new format of the agreement.

[TATA_old_KFS.pdf](TATA%20KFS%20and%20Agreement%20Phase%201/TATA_old_KFS.pdf)

[TATA_new_KFS.pdf](TATA%20KFS%20and%20Agreement%20Phase%201/TATA_new_KFS.pdf)

Loan Details: this section is a completely new section that will be added to the new format of the agreement.

Old KFS: Provides placeholders like loan_amount for fields like loan amount, total interest, processing fees etc.

New KFS: Includes the actual values for the loan details, e.g., loan amount of Rs. 2,50,000, processing fees of Rs. 1,000 etc. This is in the format prescribed by RBI.

- Interest Rate:

Old KFS: Simply states the rate of interest as rate_of_interest%.
New KFS: Provides a detailed breakdown of the interest rate, specifying it as floating rate of 10.50%, based on TCL-STPLR of 9.60% + Spread of 0.90%. Also mentions the reset frequency.

- Other Fees & Charges:

Old KFS: Has placeholders for other charges like service charges, credit verification charges, and lien mark charges, to be paid as actual.
New KFS: Provides the actual amounts for charges like Rs. 200 stamp duty, Rs. 999 pledge creation charges. Also includes additional charges like cheque dishonor fees, annual renewal fees etc. which were not captured in the old KFS.

- Repayment Details:

Old KFS: Includes placeholders for EMI amount, number of EMIs, and states that they vary based on utilization.
New KFS: Provides illustrative values assuming 100% utilization, with EMI of Rs. 2,187.50 for 12 months. Also includes a detailed repayment schedule.

- Additional Sections:

Old KFS: Does not include separate sections for penal charges, grievance redressal, or clauses related to loan transfer and recovery agents.
New KFS: Has detailed sections covering penal charges (e.g., 3% p.m. for delayed payment), grievance redressal mechanism with contact details of grievance officer, and explicit Y/N field for whether the loan can be transferred to other lenders or recovery agents engaged.

- Disclosures & Disclaimers:

Old KFS: Disclaimers focus on how the values are subject to change based on utilization.
New KFS: Includes additional disclosures like the cooling-off period of 3 days, and a note that the amounts are net of taxes like GST which will be charged extra.

- Formatting & Structure:

Old KFS: Plain text table format, with placeholders for dynamic fields.
New KFS: Structured, tabular format with clear sections and actual values filled in. Also includes an Annual Percentage Rate (APR) calculation which was missing earlier.

---

# **Design**

---

No design change is required in this phase. In the subsequent phase, we will modify the KFS UI.

# **Analytics**

---

Below are the metrics we will review.

- TAT for loading the agreement
- TAT for generating the agreement
- Agreement generation success %age
- Agreement acceptance %age
